Title: promotionCoupons — Happy Path Query
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. Store {{STORE_ID}} has at least one active+public promotion with a coupon code. Asserts the public xMarketing query returns a connection-style response with non-null couponCode on every item (filter contract).
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=list_coupons] errors[] empty
[DATA label=list_coupons] data.promotionCoupons is non-null
[COUNT label=list_coupons] data.promotionCoupons.totalCount >= 1
[DATA label=list_coupons] data.promotionCoupons.items.0.id is non-null
[DATA label=list_coupons] data.promotionCoupons.items.0.systemName is non-null
[DATA label=list_coupons] data.promotionCoupons.items.0.couponCode is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] All returned items have couponCode != null — promotions without coupons must be excluded by the resolver (BL-PRICE-001) — verify by scanning items[] in evidence JSON
[EVIDENCE] endDate is ISO-8601 string when present; null acceptable for open-ended promotions
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty on happy path; couponCode null on any returned item (filter regression); totalCount = 0 in a store known to have couponed promotions

Title: promotionCoupons — Schema Introspection Contract
Preconditions: Runner-native. Public introspection — runner's client-side validator already enforces this contract; this test asserts the schema discoverability of promotionCoupons via __type so a regression that drops the field surfaces in errors[].
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=introspect_query] errors[] empty
[DATA label=introspect_query] data.__type is non-null
[DATA label=introspect_query] data.__type.fields is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] Scan data.__type.fields[] for name="promotionCoupons" — must be present (BL-GQL-001) — verify in evidence JSON
[EVIDENCE] Inspect promotionCoupons args[]: storeId is NON_NULL String; userId, currencyCode, cultureName, first, after, keyword, sort are nullable — verify in evidence JSON
Failure_Signals: promotionCoupons field absent from Query type; storeId arg is nullable instead of NON_NULL String!; introspection returns 5xx

Title: promotionCoupons — Anonymous Caller Is Hard-Gated (Unauthorized)
Preconditions: Runner-native — NO [AUTH] block, query runs without Bearer token. Live verification (2026-04-27, B2B-store) confirmed promotionCoupons is HARD-GATED: anonymous request returns errors[].extensions.code="Unauthorized" + data.promotionCoupons=null + HTTP 200 (GraphQL error envelope, not 401). Mirrors the orders() gate pattern in 050g GQL-016. Asserts the resolver does NOT silently return data and does NOT leak internal details.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=anon_query] errors[] non-empty — anonymous request rejected with Unauthorized extension code (BL-GQL-004)
[DATA label=anon_query] data.promotionCoupons is null
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] errors[0].extensions.code = "Unauthorized" — hard auth-gate (BL-GQL-004) — verify in evidence JSON
[EVIDENCE] errors[0].path includes "promotionCoupons" — error scoped to the gated resolver
[EVIDENCE] No stack traces, file paths, SQL fragments, internal user ids, or coupon data in any error message (BL-AUTH-005) — verify in evidence JSON
Failure_Signals: errors[] empty on anonymous call (silent gate bypass — security failure); data.promotionCoupons returns real coupon items without auth (cross-tenant leak); error message exposes server internals; HTTP 5xx
References: xAPI; graphql-schema.md — Query.promotionCoupons; 050g GQL-016 noauth_orders pattern; VCST-4590

Title: promotionCoupons — Wrong Store Returns Forbidden
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated against {{STORE_ID}} — the test queries a non-existent store id ("STORE_DOES_NOT_EXIST"). Live verification (2026-04-27, B2B-store) confirmed the resolver returns errors[].extensions.code="Forbidden" + data.promotionCoupons=null (HTTP 200). The test guards against a regression where the resolver silently returns Store A data or leaks Store B coupon codes in the error message.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=wrong_store] errors[] non-empty — wrong-store request rejected with Forbidden extension code (BL-GQL-004)
[DATA label=wrong_store] data.promotionCoupons is null
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] errors[0].extensions.code = "Forbidden" — store-scoping authorization gate (BL-AUTH-005)
[EVIDENCE] errors[0].path includes "promotionCoupons" — error scoped to the gated resolver
[EVIDENCE] No promotion id, systemName, name, label, description, or couponCode appears in any error message (cross-store leak guard) — verify in evidence JSON
Failure_Signals: Real promotion data returned for a non-existent / unauthorized store (silent gate bypass); errors[] contains item ids or coupon codes from another store; HTTP 5xx

Title: promotionCoupons — Missing Required storeId Argument (Schema Validation)
Preconditions: Runner-native. Server schema introspection cached; runner validates client-side before send so omitting the required NON_NULL storeId surfaces a DV-008/DV-010 schema-validation error in errors[] with data=null — mirrors the structured GraphQL-error contract.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=missing_storeid] errors[] non-empty — promotionCoupons requires storeId argument (DV-008/DV-010) (BL-GQL-001)
[DATA label=missing_storeid] data is null
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] Error message references the missing argument name "storeId" — verify in evidence JSON
[EVIDENCE] No stack traces, internal paths, or SQL fragments in error message — verify in evidence JSON
Failure_Signals: errors[] empty when required arg is missing (silent acceptance); data returned despite missing required arg; error exposes server internals
References: xAPI; graphql-schema.md — error contract & DV-* codes; VCST-4590

Title: promotionCoupons — Pagination Correctness (first/after)
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. Store {{STORE_ID}} has 3+ active+public couponed promotions. Two queries paginate via first:2 + after:"0" then after:"2" — these are numeric offset strings, not cursor values. The promotionCoupons resolver currently accepts numeric offsets in the after arg; cursor-based pagination via pageInfo.endCursor is tracked as gap GAP-001 (see TLC-2026-04-27-1700). Test asserts no overlap and consistent totalCount.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=page1] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=page2] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=page1] data.promotionCoupons.items.length <= 2
[COUNT label=page2] data.promotionCoupons.items.length <= 2
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] {{P1_TOTAL}} = {{P2_TOTAL}} — totalCount stable across pages (BL-GQL-002) — verify in evidence JSON
[EVIDENCE] {{P2_ITEM0_ID}} != {{P1_ITEM0_ID}} AND {{P2_ITEM0_ID}} != {{P1_ITEM1_ID}} — no duplicate item between pages
Failure_Signals: totalCount fluctuates between sequential page queries; same item id appears on both pages (pagination broken); errors[] non-empty on either page

Title: promotionCoupons — Filter Excludes Inactive and Private Promotions
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. Environment must contain (a) at least one inactive promotion (isActive=false), (b) at least one private promotion (isPublic=false), and (c) at least one active+public+couponed promotion in {{STORE_ID}}. The query asks for first:100 and the test asserts the resolver only returns the qualifying subset.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=filter_query] errors[] empty
[DATA label=filter_query] data.promotionCoupons.items is non-null
[COUNT label=filter_query] data.promotionCoupons.totalCount >= 1
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] Every items[].couponCode is non-null — no promotion-without-coupon leak (BL-PRICE-001)
[EVIDENCE] Cross-check Admin Marketing → Promotions filter (Active=true AND Public=true AND coupon-count>=1) for {{STORE_ID}} — count must equal data.promotionCoupons.totalCount (runner-manual evidence)
Failure_Signals: Inactive (isActive=false) or private (isPublic=false) promotion appears in items[] (filter regression); totalCount inflated by non-qualifying promotions

Title: promotionCoupons — Localization (cultureName en-US vs Default)
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. Promotion exists with en-US translations for name/label/description. Two paired queries (with and without cultureName) verify the resolver returns localized strings only when cultureName is provided and that systemName is locale-independent.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=with_culture] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=without_culture] errors[] empty
[DATA label=with_culture] data.promotionCoupons.items.0.systemName is non-null
[DATA label=without_culture] data.promotionCoupons.items.0.systemName is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] {{ITEM_SYSNAME}} = {{ITEM_SYSNAME_NO_CULTURE}} — systemName is locale-independent (BL-GQL-002)
[EVIDENCE] {{ITEM_NAME_ENUS}} non-null when cultureName=en-US AND a translation exists for the promotion — verify in evidence JSON
[EVIDENCE] {{ITEM_NAME_NO_CULTURE}} is either null or a non-empty default-locale string — resolver MUST NOT crash or return a mismatched translation; if a value is returned it must differ from the en-US localized value when distinct translations exist (verify in evidence JSON)
Failure_Signals: systemName differs between cultureName modes; localized name/label/description identical regardless of cultureName (translation lookup broken); empty string instead of null for missing locale

Title: promotionCoupons — Cross-Layer: Admin Deactivate Removes Promotion From Response
Preconditions: Semi-automated. ORG_USER authenticated. Phase 1 captures baseline totalCount + ids via runner. Phase 2 (manual) — operator toggles a target promotion to inactive in Admin (Marketing → Promotions blade). Phase 3 re-queries via runner and asserts the deactivated id is gone and totalCount decremented by 1.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=baseline] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=after_deactivate] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=baseline] data.promotionCoupons.totalCount >= 1
[DATA label=after_deactivate] data.promotionCoupons is non-null
[DATA label=after_deactivate] data.promotionCoupons.totalCount < {{BASELINE_TOTAL}}
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] Exact decrement: {{POST_TOTAL}} = {{BASELINE_TOTAL}} - 1 — runner-MVP cannot express arithmetic on captured vars (pending MATH support in runner v2); the [DATA] strict-less-than assertion above covers the dominant regression mode where deactivation has no effect (BL-PRICE-001)
[EVIDENCE] after_deactivate.data.promotionCoupons.items[] does NOT contain {{DEACTIVATED_ID}} — runner-MVP has no array-not-contains predicate; verify by scanning items[] in evidence JSON
Failure_Signals: Deactivated promotion still appears in items[] (cache staleness or filter regression); totalCount unchanged after Admin toggle (caught by [DATA] strict-less-than assertion); errors[] non-empty on re-query
Cleanup: Operator: re-activate {{DEACTIVATED_ID}} in Admin Marketing → Promotions to restore environment state

Title: promotionCoupons — E2E: Apply Returned CouponCode in Cart
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. Phase 1 reads a real coupon code from promotionCoupons. Phase 2 builds a cart (clear → addItem) and applies the captured code via addCoupon mutation. Asserts isAppliedSuccessfully=true and discountTotal > 0.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=setup_coupons] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=apply_coupon] errors[] empty — coupon applied without server error
[DATA label=apply_coupon] data.addCoupon.coupons is non-null
[COUNT label=apply_coupon] data.addCoupon.coupons.length >= 1
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] data.addCoupon.coupons[] contains an entry with code = {{COUPON_CODE}} AND isAppliedSuccessfully = true (BL-CART-003)
[EVIDENCE] data.addCoupon.discountTotal.amount > 0 — coupon produced a real discount (BL-PRICE-001)
[EVIDENCE] data.addCoupon.total.amount = data.addCoupon.subTotal.amount - data.addCoupon.discountTotal.amount (± shipping/tax) — math consistency
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty on addCoupon (mutation rejected); isAppliedSuccessfully=false despite the code coming directly from promotionCoupons (contract mismatch); discountTotal=0 after apply
Cleanup: [GQL-OP cleanup_clear] mutation { clearCart(command: { storeId: \"{{STORE_ID}}\" userId: \"{{USER_ID}}\" }) { id } } [GQL-EXEC cleanup_clear]

Title: promotionCoupons — Error Schema: No Internal Data Leak
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. Two probes: (a) unknown field on items[] (DV-009 client validation), (b) very large `first` value to surface any timeout / capping behavior. Asserts errors[] is structured and contains no stack traces, file paths, or SQL fragments.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=unknown_field] errors[] non-empty — unknown field INVALID_FIELD on PromotionCouponType (DV-009) (BL-GQL-001)
[DATA label=unknown_field] data is null
[ERRORS label=large_first] errors[] empty — server returns full result set without error or cap
[DATA label=large_first] data.promotionCoupons.totalCount >= 1
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] unknown_field error message references the field name "INVALID_FIELD" by name — verify in evidence JSON
[EVIDENCE] No stack traces, file paths, connection strings, or SQL fragments in any error message (BL-AUTH-005) — verify in evidence JSON
[PERF label=large_first] elapsed_ms target < 2000ms (no timeout on absurd first value)
Failure_Signals: errors[] empty on obviously invalid query (silent failure); error message includes server stack trace or internal path; large_first query times out or returns 5xx
References: xAPI; graphql-schema.md — error contract & DV-* codes; VCST-4590

Title: promotionCoupons — Performance: Response Time Stability
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. Three identical queries — runner captures elapsed_ms per execution in evidence JSON. Asserts no errors and stable totalCount across runs; performance thresholds noted as [PERF] tags for CI-side evaluation.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=perf_run1] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=perf_run2] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=perf_run3] errors[] empty
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] {{RUN1_TOTAL}} = {{RUN2_TOTAL}} = {{RUN3_TOTAL}} — totalCount stable (no flapping) (BL-GQL-002)
[PERF label=perf_run1] elapsed_ms target < 500ms (captured in evidence)
[PERF label=perf_run2] elapsed_ms target < 500ms

Title: promotionCoupons — Response-Time Consistency Across Repeated Calls
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. The runner repeats the same query 5x (with first:10) and captures elapsed_ms each time. These are sequential top-level queries — not a DataLoader batching test (DataLoader operates within a single request's field resolution). The test detects response-time consistency across repeated calls: flat times indicate stable server-side performance (no cold-start penalty or progressive degradation). Renamed scope: response-time consistency, not DataLoader N+1 detection.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=n1_run1] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=n1_run2] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=n1_run3] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=n1_run4] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=n1_run5] errors[] empty
[DATA label=n1_run1] data.promotionCoupons.items is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] All items[].couponCode are non-null on every run — DataLoader resolved couponCode field correctly (BL-GQL-002)
[PERF label=n1_run1] elapsed_ms target < 1000ms for 10 items
[PERF] N+1 detection (runner-v2): elapsed_ms across the 5 runs should be roughly flat — review evidence for proportional growth
Failure_Signals: Any couponCode null on any run (DataLoader miss); response time grows proportionally with repetition (N+1 regression); errors[] non-empty on any run
